root_text.an4.14:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā na nimittaggāhī hoti nānubyañjanaggāhī.
root_text.an4.14:1.6: Yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ cakkhundriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya paṭipajjati, rakkhati cakkhundriyaṁ, cakkhundriye saṁvaraṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an4.14:1.11: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya na nimittaggāhī hoti nānubyañjanaggāhī,
root_text.an4.14:1.12: yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ manindriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya paṭipajjati, rakkhati manindriyaṁ, manindriye saṁvaraṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an4.14:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ kāmavitakkaṁ nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti;
root_text.an4.14:2.5: uppannuppanne pāpake akusale dhamme nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.an4.14:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ,
root_text.an4.14:3.8: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.an4.14:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ bhaddakaṁ samādhinimittaṁ anurakkhati aṭṭhikasaññaṁ puḷavakasaññaṁ vinīlakasaññaṁ vicchiddakasaññaṁ uddhumātakasaññaṁ.

translation_text.an4.14:1.3: The efforts to restrain, to give up, to develop, and to preserve.
translation_text.an4.14:1.4: And what, mendicants, is the effort to restrain?
translation_text.an4.14:1.5: When a mendicant sees a sight with their eyes, they don’t get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an4.14:1.6: If the faculty of sight were left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure would become overwhelming. For this reason, they practice restraint, protecting the faculty of sight, and achieving its restraint.
translation_text.an4.14:1.7: When they hear a sound with their ears …
translation_text.an4.14:1.8: When they smell an odor with their nose …
translation_text.an4.14:1.9: When they taste a flavor with their tongue …
translation_text.an4.14:1.10: When they feel a touch with their body …
translation_text.an4.14:1.11: When they know an idea with their mind, they don’t get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an4.14:1.12: If the faculty of mind were left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure would become overwhelming. For this reason, they practice restraint, protecting the faculty of mind, and achieving its restraint.
translation_text.an4.14:1.13: This is called the effort to restrain.
translation_text.an4.14:2.1: And what, mendicants, is the effort to give up?
translation_text.an4.14:2.2: It’s when a mendicant doesn’t tolerate a sensual,
translation_text.an4.14:2.4: or cruel thought that’s arisen, but gives it up, gets rid of it, eliminates it, and obliterates it.
translation_text.an4.14:2.5: They don’t tolerate any bad, unskillful qualities that have arisen, but give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them.
translation_text.an4.14:2.6: This is called the effort to give up.
translation_text.an4.14:3.1: And what, mendicants, is the effort to develop?
translation_text.an4.14:3.2: It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness,
translation_text.an4.14:3.8: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.an4.14:3.9: This is called the effort to develop.
translation_text.an4.14:4.1: And what, mendicants, is the effort to preserve?
translation_text.an4.14:4.2: It’s when a mendicant preserves a meditation subject that’s a fine basis of immersion: the perception of a skeleton, a worm-infested corpse, a livid corpse, a split open corpse, or a bloated corpse.
translation_text.an4.14:4.3: This is called the effort to preserve.
translation_text.an4.14:5.4: taught by the Kinsman of the Sun.
translation_text.an4.14:5.5: Any mendicant who keenly applies these
translation_text.an4.14:5.6: may attain the ending of suffering.”

reference_text.an4.14:1.1: cck21.21, ms15A4_98, msdiv14, pts-vp-pli2.16, sya21.21, vri36.19
